fact.fact: The Indonesian government's state revenue from the energy and mineral resources sector (PNBP) was 249 trillion IDR in 2025, which is 98.03% of the target of 254 trillion IDR.

verdict.reason: The source text directly states the PNBP revenue for 2025 as 249 trillion IDR and the percentage of the target as 98.03%.

fact.fact: The Indonesian government's state revenue from the energy and mineral resources sector (PNBP) was 268 trillion IDR in 2024, which is 118.06% of the target of 227 trillion IDR.

fact.fact: The Indonesia Crude Price (ICP) was 68.79 USD/Barrel as of 06 Apr 2026.

synthesis.content: The Indonesian government's state revenue from the energy and mineral resources sector has varied, with 249 trillion IDR in 2025 and 268 trillion IDR in 2024, compared to targets of 254 trillion IDR and 227 trillion IDR respectively. The Electrification Ratio in Indonesia was 99.83% as of 06 Apr 2026. Oil and gas production in Indonesia were 575,245 barrels per day and 6,936 million standard cubic feet per day, respectively, as of 08 Apr 2026, with the Indonesia Crude Price at 68.79 USD/Barrel as of 06 Apr 2026.

The Electrification Ratio in Indonesia was 99.83% as of 06 Apr 2026. The Indonesian government's state revenue from the energy and mineral resources sector has fluctuated, with 268 trillion IDR in 2024 and 249 trillion IDR in 2025, against targets of 227 trillion IDR and 254 trillion IDR respectively. As of 06 Apr 2026, the Electrification Ratio in Indonesia was 99.83%. The Oil Production in Indonesia was 575,245 barrels per day as of 08 Apr 2026, while the Gas Production was 6,936 million standard cubic feet per day, with the Indonesia Crude Price at 68.79 USD/Barrel as of 06 Apr 2026. These metrics provide insight into the country's energy sector performance.
